subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: rebuilt from independent sources (IMF, Freedom House, RSF, V-Dem, Crisis Group) + exile/international press (RFE/RL, turkmen.news, Eurasianet); state media EXCLUDED.

Data limitation: extraordinarily opaque - even IMF figures rely on authorities' data and most social indicators come from exile media; confidence is deliberately reduced across the board and no dramatic single-source claim is encoded as fact.

Standout drags: one of the world's most closed/repressive states under a Berdimuhamedov dynastic duopoly, a captive single-buyer (China) gas economy behind a ~5-6x black-market manat gap and strict capital controls, and RSF near-bottom (173rd).

economic.notes: Low headline debt but a captive single-buyer gas model behind a rationed, non-convertible currency.

fiscal_state.notes: Very low public debt (~3.6% of GDP, 2024) and a current-account surplus, but opaque off-budget funds and a poor fiscal-transparency record. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: The manat is fixed at 3.5/USD since 2015 with a multiple-rate regime; the central bank has no autonomy and FX is administratively rationed. (IMF; Eurasianet)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The manat is not a reserve currency and is non-convertible. (definitional)

inflation.notes: The IMF reports sharp disinflation (~1.1% in early 2025), but the official series is widely doubted given suppressed prices and shortages. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

banking_stability.notes: A state-dominated, largely closed banking system with heavy FX rationing and little independent visibility. (IMF; trade.gov)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: World's 4th-largest gas reserves but a captive single-buyer model (China ~80%+), with TAPI essentially stalled. (IMF; Eurasianet; The Diplomat)

institutional.notes: Among the world's least-free states, with near-total data opacity and an information desert.

rule_of_law.notes: Among the world's least-free - no judicial independence, systemic corruption, and a total absence of political rights. (Freedom House 2025/26 'Not Free')

statistical_integrity.notes: One of the most data-opaque countries on earth - economic data IMF-admitted but unverifiable, most social statistics absent. (IMF; V-Dem)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A patronage-and-loyalty bureaucracy around a personality cult, with purges and weak delivery outside showcase projects. (Freedom House; Crisis Group)

press_freedom.notes: RSF 173/180 (2026), last globally on political context - virtually no independent media and heavy foreign-internet blocking. (RSF 2026)

political_social.notes: Totalitarian near-term stability over enforced homogeneity, food shortages, and out-migration.

political_stability.notes: Repression delivers near-term surface stability under the dynastic duopoly, but an unaccountable two-headed succession and economic stress make the long horizon brittle. (Freedom House; Crisis Group)

civil_liberties.notes: Almost no freedom of movement, assembly, religion, or expression, with reports of forced loyalty rituals. (Freedom House 2025; CIVICUS)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Not a migrant destination; foreigners face severe movement/exit controls, and emigration itself is restricted. (Freedom House; RFE/RL)

social_cohesion.notes: Enforced homogeneity and a personality cult mask grievances, with food shortages and rolled-back subsidies adding strain. (Freedom House; Borgen)

demographics.notes: Relatively young but with heavy out-migration of working-age citizens and unreliable census data. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: An isolationist 'positive neutral' reliable only as a gas supplier to China, with draconian capital controls.

alliance_reliability.notes: A 'positively neutral' non-aligned isolationist, reliable only as a gas supplier to China, with no real alliance commitments. (Crisis Group; The Diplomat)

conflict_involvement.notes: Largely avoids external conflict despite an Afghanistan border, insulated by neutrality. (Crisis Group)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Not broadly sanctioned, but among the world's most restrictive capital-control/FX-rationing regimes, making repatriation extremely difficult. (IMF; trade.gov)

physical_practical.notes: Desert heat and acute water stress, with gas-funded showcase projects over decaying provincial utilities.

climate.notes: Extreme desert heat, acute water scarcity (Amu Darya/Aral), aridification, very low readiness and opaque governance. (IPCC AR6; WB)

healthcare.notes: Life expectancy ~70 but a documented public-health crisis, official denial of outbreaks, and a collapsed information system. (WHO data; Open Society) - low confidence

infrastructure.notes: Lavish gas-funded Ashgabat showcase projects against decaying provincial utilities and rolled-back subsidies. (Eurasianet; Borgen)

crime_safety.notes: Low reported street crime in a pervasive police state - figures are state-controlled and unverifiable. (soft/indirect)

disaster_insurance.notes: Seismic exposure (Ashgabat on a major fault) with negligible private insurance penetration. (seismic-hazard literature)

summary: Among the most closed and repressive states on the planet, run as a personality-cult dynastic duopoly in which President Serdar Berdimuhamedov is subordinate to his father Gurbanguly's People's Council. Its economy rests almost entirely on gas exports to a single buyer (China), behind a fixed manat that trades at a ~5-6x black-market premium under draconian capital controls, with TAPI diversification still stalled. Headline IMF data (very low debt, sub-2% inflation) look benign but are unverifiable amid near-total information control, food shortages, and a public-health crisis. Every score carries a heavy opacity discount; the long-run risk is a brittle dynastic succession layered over Caspian/desert climate stress.
